7.reason: [This loop reads all input as integer pairs, ignoring the actual graph structure and delete vertex. It does not perform DFS or BFS traversals as required.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This loop reads all input as integer pairs, ignoring the actual graph structure and delete vertex. It does not perform DFS or BFS traversals as required.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The loop reads pairs of integers but the input format consists of n, m, then m edges (each an edge pair), then a single delete vertex. This loop will either read too many or too few numbers and never terminate correctly. Also it does not stop after reading the delete vertex, so it cannot process the actual DFS/BFS traversal.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incorrect input handling; should read n and m once, then read m edges and the target vertex, rather than looping until EOF.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incorrectly uses an EOF loop and only reads n and m, completely ignoring the input of m edges and the vertex to delete.

9.reason: [This prints the raw input pairs instead of the required vertex sequences for DFS and BFS before and after deletion.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This prints the raw input pairs instead of the required vertex sequences for DFS and BFS before and after deletion.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This prints the raw input pairs, not the required traversal sequences (DFS and BFS before and after deletion). The program must output vertex orderings, not just echo the input.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Fails to implement graph construction, DFS, BFS, or vertex deletion; only prints n and m instead of the required traversal sequences.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Simply echoes the input values instead of performing DFS and BFS traversals to output the required vertex sequences.
